items.title: Nvidia earnings: Wall Street sighs with relief after AI wave doesn’t crash
items.description: Amid a blackout of data due to the government shutdown, the $5tn chipmaker’s report took on wider significance

items.title: Nvidia ‘Committed to Engagement’ With the U.S., China
items.description: Nvidia Chief Financial Officer Colette Kress said the company is disappointed with regulatory hurdles it faces to send computing products to China and said the company would continue to engage with U.S. and Chinese governments.  “To establish a sustainable leadership and position in AI computing, America must win the support of every developer and be the platform of choice for every commercial business, including those in China,” she said.  Earlier this week, ​​Amazon.com joined Microsoft in supporting legislation that threatens to further limit Nvidia’s ability to export to China.

items.title: Nvidia Earnings Send Stocks Higher
items.description: Exchange traded funds tracking the tech sector and the S&P 500 and select tech stocks were rising in after-hours trading after Nvidia's earnings. The Invesco QQQ fund was up 1%, while the SPDR S&P 500 ETF was up 0.

items.title: Clio Completes $1B vLex Acquisition, Secures $500M At $5B Valuation In Largest Legal Tech Deal
items.description: Legal technology company Clio announced on Nov. 10 that it has closed what it calls one of the industry’s most significant and largest transactions to date. The firm closed its $1 billion purchase of vLex while raising $500 million in a Series G round that valued the company at $5 billion. New Enterprise Associates led the Series G investment, joined by existing backers Technology Crossover Ventures, Goldman Sachs Asset Management, Sixth Street Growth, and JMI Equity. items.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq rise as stocks pause sell-off streak before Nvidia earnings
items.description: Investors prepared for Nvidia earnings, following several days of losses for stocks.
